chapter_context.summary: David gathers a band of distressed and indebted men at the cave of Adullam; Saul orders Doeg the Edomite to massacre 85 priests at Nob.

outline.text: David at Adullam and Mizpeh; Joined by Outlaws
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David at Adullam and Mizpeh; Joined by Outlaws (22:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

outline.text: Saul Rebukes His Servants; Doeg Massacres the Priests of Nob
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Saul Rebukes His Servants; Doeg Massacres the Priests of Nob (22:6-19), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

outline.text: Abiathar Escapes to David with the Ephod
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Abiathar Escapes to David with the Ephod (22:20-23), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

key_themes.title: Sovereignty of God
key_themes.description: God raises up the humble (Hannah, Samuel, David) and humbles the proud (Eli's sons, Saul).

key_themes.title: The Heart vs. Outward Appearance
key_themes.description: God looks at the inner spiritual character, not physical stature or social status.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 22, how does this encourage you to trust God when circumstances look bleak?
How does this chapter prompt you to examine the sincerity of your own heart before the LORD?

cultural_historical.title: Transition to Monarchy
cultural_historical.text: Israel's request for a king represents a major cultural shift from a tribal league led by temporary judges to a centralized dynastic monarchy.
